original_hypothesis.title: Watchdog Unsuspend Action Bypasses Merkle Proof Verification
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The suspendMessages function sets proofReceipt[msgHash].receivedAt to block.timestamp when unsuspending a message. In processMessage, the condition isMessageProven = receivedAt != 0 is used to skip Merkle proof verification. A compromised watchdog can suspend and immediately unsuspend any arbitrary message hash, allowing an attacker to process completely fabricated cross-chain messages without valid proofs.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a catastrophic logic flaw that elevates the watchdog's pause privilege into a full proof-bypass capability. If the watchdog key is compromised, the attacker can drain all ETH from the bridge and mint infinite bridged tokens from the vaults by executing fake messages that were never sent on the source chain.
preconditions: Watchdog key is compromised or acts maliciously
Watchdog suspends and then unsuspends a fabricated message hash

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: isMessageProven evaluates to true for unproven hashes if unsuspended
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Attacker processes fake messages to drain bridge and vaults
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Attacker steals 100% of TVL in the bridge and all connected token vaults

economic_questions: Can this be executed atomically in a single block?
Is there any secondary validation that would catch the fake message?
validation_requirements: Simulate watchdog suspending and unsuspending a fake hash
Call processMessage with the fake message payload and verify it succeeds without a proof

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: All users with funds in the bridge or vaults
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Total Value Locked (TVL) of the protocol

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
